hub.title: What We Do — AI Consultancy Services & Solutions
hub.description: AI readiness assessments, agentic AI systems, automation, governance, and training. We build and deploy AI that delivers measurable results for UK businesses.

pages.name: AI Readiness Assessment
pages.title: AI Readiness Assessment — Agentic AI Associates
pages.description: Fixed-fee AI readiness assessment for UK businesses. We audit your data, processes, and team capability, then deliver a prioritised roadmap with clear ROI projections.
pages.intro: Most AI projects fail because nobody checked whether the business was ready. Our AI Readiness Assessment is a fixed-fee, fixed-scope engagement that gives you a clear picture of where you stand — and exactly where to start.

sections.heading: What the Assessment Covers
sections.body:
We evaluate four areas: your data maturity (what data you have, where it lives, how clean it is), your technology infrastructure (what systems you run, what APIs are available, what's legacy), your team capability (who has AI skills, what gaps exist), and your process landscape (which workflows are candidates for AI automation).

The output is not a generic report. It's a scored matrix of AI opportunities specific to your business, ranked by impact and feasibility. Each opportunity includes estimated ROI, timeline, and the resources needed to deliver it.

sections.heading: How It Works
sections.body:
The assessment runs over two to four weeks. Week one: we interview your leadership and frontline teams, review your existing technology stack, and map your key business processes. Week two: we analyse the findings, score opportunities, and build your personalised roadmap.

You receive a presentation walkthrough plus a written report. No dusty PDF — a living document your team can act on immediately. If you want us to help execute the roadmap, we scope that as a separate engagement based on the assessment findings.

sections.heading: Who This Is For
sections.body:
The assessment suits two types of business. First: organisations that know AI matters but haven't started. You need an honest, expert view of what's possible before committing budget. Second: organisations that have tried AI but got stuck — perhaps a pilot that never reached production, or multiple teams buying different tools with no coordination.

We work across financial services, healthcare, retail, manufacturing, legal, and professional services. Company size typically ranges from 50 to 500 employees.

sections.heading: What You Walk Away With
sections.body:
A scored AI opportunity matrix with estimated ROI for each use case. A technology gap analysis showing what infrastructure changes are needed. A team skills assessment with training recommendations. And a phased roadmap covering the next 12 to 24 months, broken into quarterly sprints with clear deliverables.

Every recommendation ties back to a business outcome — cost reduction, revenue growth, or operational efficiency. No vanity projects.

features.title: Data Maturity Assessment
features.description: A structured evaluation of your data assets, quality, governance, and accessibility. Benchmarked against your industry.

features.title: AI Opportunity Matrix
features.description: Every potential AI use case scored by business impact, technical feasibility, and time to value.

features.title: Technology Gap Analysis
features.description: Assessment of your current infrastructure against what's needed for AI deployment.

features.title: Team Skills Audit
features.description: Mapping of existing AI capabilities and identification of training or hiring needs.

features.title: Phased Roadmap
features.description: A 12 to 24 month plan broken into quarterly sprints with clear deliverables and decision gates.

features.title: Board-Ready Business Case
features.description: ROI projections, cost breakdowns, and risk analysis formatted for senior decision-makers.

faq.question: How much does the AI Readiness Assessment cost?
faq.answer: The assessment is a fixed-fee engagement. Contact us for current pricing — it depends on the size and complexity of your organisation, but we always provide a clear price upfront with no hidden costs.

faq.question: How long does the assessment take?
faq.answer: Typically two to four weeks from kickoff to final presentation. Larger organisations with multiple departments may take slightly longer.

faq.question: Do we need to prepare anything?
faq.answer: We'll send you a short pre-assessment questionnaire. Beyond that, we just need access to your key stakeholders for interviews and a view of your existing technology stack.

faq.question: What happens after the assessment?
faq.answer: You receive a complete report with prioritised recommendations. If you want us to help implement the roadmap, we scope that as a separate engagement based on the findings. There's no obligation to continue.

faq.question: Is this suitable for businesses that have already started with AI?
faq.answer: Yes. Many of our assessments are for organisations that have run pilots or adopted individual tools but lack a coordinated strategy. We help untangle what exists and create a unified plan.

pages.name: AI Strategy & Roadmaps
pages.title: AI Strategy & Roadmaps — Agentic AI Associates
pages.description: Expert AI strategy consulting. We build clear roadmaps that align AI with your business goals, budget, and timeline. UK-based.
pages.intro: Most AI projects fail because there was never a proper plan. We help you build an AI strategy that starts with your actual business problems, not the latest tech hype.

sections.heading: Why You Need an AI Strategy Before You Build Anything
sections.body:
Jumping straight into AI tools without a strategy is like renovating a house without blueprints. You might get lucky, but you will almost certainly waste money and end up with something that does not fit together.

A proper AI strategy starts with understanding where your business actually loses time and money. We audit your operations, talk to your teams, and identify the three to five areas where AI will deliver the fastest, most measurable return.

sections.heading: Our Strategy Process
sections.body:
We follow a four-phase approach. Phase one is discovery — we spend time with your leadership and frontline teams to understand workflows, pain points, and existing technology. Phase two is opportunity mapping — we match your challenges against proven AI capabilities and score each by impact and feasibility.

Phase three is roadmap design, where we build a phased plan covering technology choices, data requirements, team skills, and budget. Phase four is the governance framework — the decision-making structure, KPIs, and review cadence to keep your AI programme on track.

sections.heading: Who This Is For
sections.body:
Organisations that know AI matters but have not started and need a clear-eyed assessment of what is possible, what it costs, and where to begin. Or organisations that have started but feel stuck — perhaps a proof of concept worked but never reached production, or multiple teams are buying different tools with no coordination.

We work across sectors including finance, healthcare, retail, and manufacturing. Company size typically ranges from 50 to 500 employees.

sections.heading: What You Walk Away With
sections.body: An AI maturity assessment, a prioritised opportunity register with estimated ROI, a 12 to 24 month roadmap broken into quarterly sprints, technology and vendor recommendations, and a risk register with change management plan.

features.title: AI Maturity Assessment
features.description: Structured evaluation of your current data, technology, skills, and culture.

features.title: Opportunity Scoring Matrix
features.description: Every AI use case scored by business impact, technical feasibility, and time to value.

features.title: Phased Roadmap
features.description: A 12 to 24 month plan broken into quarterly sprints with clear deliverables.

features.title: Technology Guidance
features.description: Independent recommendations on platforms, tools, and partners.

features.title: Board-Ready Business Case
features.description: ROI projections and risk analysis for senior decision-makers.

features.title: Change Management Plan
features.description: Practical plan covering communication, training, and role impact.

faq.question: How long does an AI strategy engagement take?
faq.answer: Typically 2 to 6 weeks depending on the size and complexity of your organisation.

faq.question: Do we need existing AI capability?
faq.answer: No. Many clients start from zero. The strategy engagement is designed to take you from where you are to where you need to be.

faq.question: What does an AI strategy cost?
faq.answer: Strategy engagements start from £3,000 for smaller organisations. Contact us for a scoped proposal.

faq.question: Will you help us implement the strategy?
faq.answer: If you want us to. Many clients use the strategy to brief internal teams or other vendors. Others ask us to lead implementation directly.

pages.name: Agentic AI Systems
pages.title: Agentic AI Consultancy UK — Autonomous AI Agents
pages.description: We design, build, and deploy agentic AI systems — autonomous agents that handle complex, multi-step business workflows with minimal human intervention.
pages.intro: Agentic AI is the next evolution beyond chatbots and copilots. These are autonomous systems that plan, execute, and adapt — handling complex workflows end to end. We build them for UK businesses.

sections.heading: What Agentic AI Actually Is
sections.body:
Agentic AI refers to systems that can take sequences of actions autonomously to complete complex, multi-step tasks. Unlike a chatbot that responds to prompts, an agentic system uses tools, browses data sources, makes decisions, and loops back to check its work.

In practice, this means automating workflows that previously required human judgement — from procurement processes that span multiple systems, to customer service escalations that need context from five different data sources.

sections.heading: How We Build Agentic Systems
sections.body:
We start by mapping the workflow you want to automate — every decision point, every data source, every exception path. Then we design an agent architecture that handles the happy path and the edge cases.

We use a combination of LLMs, tool-use frameworks, and custom orchestration to build agents that are reliable enough for production. Every system includes human-in-the-loop controls, so your team stays in charge of high-stakes decisions.

sections.heading: Where Agentic AI Delivers Most Value
sections.body:
The biggest wins come from multi-step processes that touch multiple systems — procurement, compliance monitoring, customer onboarding, financial reporting. If a workflow requires a person to log into three different tools and make judgement calls, it's a candidate for agentic automation.

We work across financial services, legal, healthcare, and manufacturing.

sections.heading: Governance and Control
sections.body:
Autonomous systems need guardrails. Every agentic system we build includes logging, monitoring, human escalation triggers, and audit trails. We design for the UK regulatory environment — GDPR, FCA, ICO — from day one.

For organisations that need a broader AI governance framework, we offer dedicated governance consulting.

features.title: Agent Architecture Design
features.description: Blueprint for autonomous agents including decision flows, tool integrations, and fallback paths.

features.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
features.description: Systems where multiple agents collaborate on complex tasks, with coordination and conflict resolution.

features.title: Human-in-the-Loop Controls
features.description: Escalation triggers, approval gates, and override mechanisms so your team stays in control.

features.title: Production-Grade Deployment
features.description: Monitoring, logging, error handling, and performance optimisation for real-world reliability.

features.title: Tool Integration
features.description: Agents that connect to your existing systems — CRM, ERP, databases, APIs, email.

features.title: Compliance-Ready Design
features.description: Audit trails, data governance, and regulatory compliance baked in from the start.

faq.question: What is the difference between agentic AI and a chatbot?
faq.answer: A chatbot responds to prompts. An agentic AI system autonomously plans and executes multi-step workflows, using tools and data sources to complete tasks without constant human direction.

faq.question: Is agentic AI safe for production use?
faq.answer: Yes, when built properly. We include human-in-the-loop controls, monitoring, and guardrails in every system. The key is designing for the failure modes, not just the happy path.

faq.question: What does an agentic AI project cost?
faq.answer: It depends on the complexity of the workflow. Simple single-agent systems start from £10,000. Multi-agent enterprise systems are scoped individually.

faq.question: How long does it take to build an agentic system?
faq.answer: A focused single-agent system can be prototyped in 2 to 4 weeks and production-ready in 6 to 8 weeks. More complex systems take longer.

pages.name: Generative AI & LLMs
pages.title: Generative AI Consultancy UK — LLMs, RAG & Content Pipelines
pages.description: We help UK businesses harness generative AI safely. Model selection, RAG systems, content pipelines, and cost optimisation.
pages.intro: Generative AI is powerful but choosing the wrong model, architecture, or approach wastes money fast. We help you build generative AI systems that actually work for your business.

sections.heading: Cutting Through the Hype
sections.body:
Not every problem needs GPT-4. We help you choose the right model for each use case — sometimes that's a frontier LLM, sometimes it's a fine-tuned smaller model, sometimes it's not AI at all. We save you from expensive mistakes.

Our approach: define the business outcome first, then select the technology that delivers it most efficiently.

sections.heading: RAG Systems and Knowledge Bases
sections.body:
Retrieval-Augmented Generation connects LLMs to your actual business data. We build RAG systems that answer questions from your documents, policies, and databases — with citations, so you can verify the output.

This is how enterprises get value from generative AI without fine-tuning models or risking hallucinated answers.

sections.heading: Content and Workflow Pipelines
sections.body: We build production pipelines for content generation, document processing, email drafting, and report creation. These are not toys — they are systems with quality controls, human review steps, and measurable throughput gains.

sections.heading: Cost Optimisation
sections.body: LLM costs escalate quickly at scale. We architect systems that use prompt caching, model routing, and tiered inference to keep costs predictable. Most clients see 40 to 60 percent cost reduction compared to naive implementations.

features.title: Model Selection & Evaluation
features.description: Independent assessment of which models fit your use cases — GPT, Claude, Gemini, open-source, or fine-tuned.

features.title: RAG Architecture
features.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems that ground LLM outputs in your actual business data.

features.title: Content Pipelines
features.description: Production systems for content generation with quality controls and human review.

features.title: Prompt Engineering
features.description: Systematic prompt design and testing to maximise output quality and consistency.

features.title: Cost Optimisation
features.description: Architecture patterns that reduce LLM costs by 40 to 60 percent at scale.

features.title: Safety & Guardrails
features.description: Output filtering, PII detection, and brand-safe content generation.

faq.question: Which LLM should we use?
faq.answer: It depends on your use case, budget, and data sensitivity requirements. We evaluate options and recommend the best fit — we are vendor neutral.

faq.question: What is RAG and do we need it?
faq.answer: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) connects an LLM to your business data so it can answer questions accurately. If you need AI that knows about your specific documents, policies, or products, yes.

faq.question: How do you handle data privacy with LLMs?
faq.answer: We design systems that keep sensitive data within your infrastructure. Options include private cloud deployments, on-premise models, and API configurations that prevent data retention.

pages.name: AI Automation & Workflows
pages.title: AI Automation Consulting UK — Intelligent Workflow Automation
pages.description: Automate repetitive tasks with AI. We design and deploy intelligent automation for UK businesses — from document processing to end-to-end workflow orchestration.
pages.intro: Most businesses lose thousands of hours each year on tasks that machines can handle faster and more accurately. We help you identify the right processes to automate and build solutions that stick.

sections.heading: What AI Automation Looks Like
sections.body:
Modern intelligent automation goes beyond simple macros. Machine learning handles tasks that previously required human judgement — invoice processing, customer onboarding, data entry, regulatory filing, lead qualification, report generation.

We work with tools like UiPath, Microsoft Power Automate, and custom Python pipelines — choosing whatever fits your existing stack.

sections.heading: How We Deliver Automation Projects
sections.body:
Every engagement starts with a process audit. We map your current workflows, identify bottlenecks, and calculate time and cost savings for each automation candidate. Then we prioritise — starting with quick wins that deliver measurable ROI within weeks.

Our delivery follows an agile approach: build, test, refine, deploy. Your team gets hands-on training, clear documentation, and ongoing support.

sections.heading: Scaling Across the Organisation
sections.body:
One successful automation project is the beginning. The real value comes when you build an automation-first culture. We help you create a Centre of Excellence — a small internal team equipped to identify and deploy new automations independently.

UK organisations that scale automation well typically see 30 to 50 percent reductions in operational costs within two years.

sections.heading: Common Automation Use Cases
sections.body: Invoice processing that cuts handling time by up to 80 percent. Employee onboarding that reduces setup from days to hours. Data entry and migration that eliminates copy-paste errors. Regulatory filing that compiles, validates, and submits documents automatically. Lead qualification that scores and routes prospects without manual review. Report generation that produces board packs and client reports on schedule.

features.title: Process Audit
features.description: Map current workflows, identify bottlenecks, and calculate savings for each automation candidate.

features.title: Quick Win Identification
features.description: Prioritised list of automations that deliver measurable ROI within weeks.

features.title: Custom Build & Integration
features.description: Solutions built to work with your existing systems — no rip and replace.

features.title: Team Training
features.description: Hands-on training so your team can operate and maintain the automation.

features.title: Centre of Excellence Setup
features.description: Governance, frameworks, and reusable components for scaling automation.

features.title: Ongoing Support
features.description: Monitoring, maintenance, and continuous improvement after deployment.

faq.question: Which processes should we automate first?
faq.answer: We start with high-volume, repetitive tasks that have clear rules. These deliver the fastest ROI with the lowest risk.

faq.question: How long before we see results?
faq.answer: Most clients see measurable results within 4 to 8 weeks. Some quick wins deliver savings within days of deployment.

faq.question: Will automation replace our staff?
faq.answer: No. The goal is to free your team from work they shouldn't be doing manually, so they can focus on higher-value activities.

sections.heading: Pipelines and Integration

sections.heading: Analytics and Machine Learning

sections.heading: Data Governance
sections.body: GDPR-compliant data governance that covers access controls, retention policies, lineage tracking, and quality monitoring. Essential for any business handling customer data in the UK.

features.title: Data Maturity Assessment

features.title: Pipeline Development

features.title: Analytics Platform

features.title: Data Governance Framework

features.title: Cloud Infrastructure

faq.question: Do we need a data strategy before starting AI?
faq.answer: In most cases, yes. AI depends on good data. A data strategy ensures you have the right data, in the right format, with the right governance.

faq.question: What if our data is a mess?
faq.answer: That is common. We start with a data audit, identify the most critical gaps, and build a phased plan to get your data AI-ready.

pages.name: Conversational AI
pages.title: Conversational AI Consultancy UK — Chatbots, Voice & NLU
pages.description: We design and build conversational AI systems — chatbots, voice assistants, and NLU systems that handle customer interactions intelligently.
pages.intro: Conversational AI has moved far beyond scripted chatbots. We build systems that understand context, handle complex queries, and know when to escalate to a human.

sections.heading: Beyond Basic Chatbots
sections.body: Modern conversational AI uses natural language understanding to handle nuanced customer interactions. We build systems that resolve queries, not just deflect them — with knowledge bases, context awareness, and seamless human handoff.

sections.heading: Omnichannel Deployment
sections.body: We deploy conversational AI across web chat, WhatsApp, SMS, voice, and internal tools. One knowledge base, multiple channels — consistent experience everywhere.

sections.heading: Measurable Customer Support Impact
sections.body: Well-implemented conversational AI handles 40 to 70 percent of first-line enquiries autonomously. That means faster response times for customers and lower cost per interaction for your business.

sections.heading: Enterprise Knowledge Bases
sections.body: We build internal copilots that answer employee questions from your policies, procedures, and documentation. Reducing time spent searching for information directly improves productivity.

features.title: NLU Design
features.description: Natural language understanding that handles real customer language, not just keywords.

features.title: Knowledge Base Integration
features.description: Connect your chatbot to your actual data — FAQs, policies, product info.

features.title: Human Handoff
features.description: Intelligent escalation when the AI reaches its limits. Context preserved for the agent.

features.title: Omnichannel Deployment
features.description: Web, WhatsApp, SMS, voice — one system, multiple channels.

features.title: Analytics Dashboard
features.description: Conversation metrics, resolution rates, and improvement insights.

features.title: Continuous Training
features.description: System that learns from interactions and improves over time.

faq.question: How is this different from a basic chatbot?
faq.answer: Basic chatbots follow scripts. Our conversational AI systems understand context, handle complex queries, access your data, and know when to bring in a human.

faq.question: What channels can you deploy to?
faq.answer: Web chat, WhatsApp, SMS, voice, Microsoft Teams, Slack — any channel your customers or staff use.

pages.name: AI Governance & Compliance
pages.title: AI Governance Consulting UK — Compliance, Ethics & Risk Frameworks
pages.description: AI governance frameworks for UK businesses. Bias audits, regulatory compliance (GDPR, AI Act), ethics policies, and risk management.
pages.intro: AI without governance is a liability. We help UK businesses build frameworks that ensure their AI systems are safe, fair, compliant, and auditable.

sections.heading: Why AI Governance Matters Now
sections.body:
The UK AI Bill and EU AI Act are creating new compliance obligations. Businesses deploying AI need documented governance frameworks, bias monitoring, and audit trails. This is not optional — it is regulatory reality.

Beyond compliance, governance protects your reputation. An AI system that makes biased decisions or handles personal data incorrectly can cause lasting damage.

sections.heading: Our Governance Framework
sections.body:
We build practical governance frameworks, not theoretical ones. This covers: AI risk assessment methodology, model documentation standards, bias detection and monitoring, data governance policies, human oversight requirements, and incident response procedures.

Every framework is tailored to your industry and regulatory environment — FCA for financial services, CQC and NHS Digital for healthcare, SRA for legal.

sections.heading: Bias Auditing
sections.body: We test your AI systems for demographic bias, data bias, and outcome bias. If we find problems, we help you fix them — through model adjustment, data rebalancing, or process redesign.

sections.heading: AI Governance for SMEs
sections.body: Enterprise-grade governance consulting typically costs £50,000 or more. We offer scaled governance frameworks for mid-market businesses — the same rigour at a price point that makes sense for organisations with 50 to 500 employees.

features.title: AI Risk Assessment
features.description: Systematic identification and scoring of risks across your AI portfolio.

features.title: Bias Audit
features.description: Testing for demographic, data, and outcome bias with remediation recommendations.

features.title: Regulatory Compliance
features.description: GDPR, UK AI Bill, EU AI Act, and sector-specific regulation (FCA, CQC, SRA).

features.title: Ethics Framework
features.description: Policies and procedures for responsible AI development and deployment.

features.title: Model Documentation
features.description: Standards for documenting AI models, training data, and decision logic.

features.title: Incident Response
features.description: Procedures for handling AI failures, bias incidents, and data breaches.

faq.question: Do we need AI governance if we only use third-party AI tools?
faq.answer: Yes. You are responsible for how AI is used in your business, even if the AI itself is provided by a third party. Governance covers usage policies, data handling, and risk management.

faq.question: What regulations apply to AI in the UK?
faq.answer: GDPR applies to all AI that processes personal data. The UK AI Bill introduces additional requirements. Sector-specific regulations (FCA, CQC, SRA) add further obligations depending on your industry.

faq.question: How much does AI governance consulting cost?
faq.answer: We offer scaled packages from £5,000 for an initial governance assessment through to £25,000+ for comprehensive frameworks. Contact us for a scoped proposal.

sections.heading: Measuring Impact
sections.body: Every training programme includes pre and post assessments so you can measure the uplift. We track adoption metrics after the workshops to ensure the training translates into actual behaviour change.

features.title: Customised Content

faq.question: Who are the workshops designed for?
faq.answer: We run sessions for all levels — from board-level awareness to technical deep-dives. Each session is tailored to the audience.

faq.question: How much do workshops cost?

faq.question: Can you deliver training remotely?

pages.intro: AI is not a one-off project. Systems need monitoring, models need retraining, and your strategy needs evolving. Our retainer packages give you ongoing access to expert support.

sections.heading: Why Ongoing Support Matters
sections.body: AI models drift. Business requirements change. New tools emerge. Without ongoing attention, AI systems that worked brilliantly at launch gradually lose effectiveness. We keep your systems performing.

sections.heading: What Retainer Support Includes
sections.body: Model monitoring and performance tracking. Retraining cycles when performance drops. Strategic advisory on new AI opportunities. Technical support for your AI systems. Quarterly roadmap reviews to align AI with evolving business goals.

sections.heading: Flexible Engagement
sections.body: Retainer packages start from £1,500 per month. We tailor the scope to your needs — some clients want weekly check-ins, others prefer monthly strategic reviews with on-demand technical support.

sections.heading: Fractional AI Leadership
sections.body: For organisations that need AI expertise but cannot justify a full-time hire, our retainer acts as a fractional AI lead. You get senior-level strategic guidance and technical capability without the overhead of a permanent role.

faq.question: What does a retainer cost?
faq.answer: Retainer packages start from £1,500 per month. We scope them based on the number of AI systems, level of support required, and frequency of engagement.

faq.question: Can we cancel the retainer?
faq.answer: Yes. Our retainers run on a rolling monthly basis with 30 days notice.

faq.question: Do you support systems you did not build?
faq.answer: Yes. We are happy to take over monitoring and optimisation of AI systems built by other teams or vendors.

sections.heading: Natural Language Processing

sections.heading: Anomaly Detection

features.description: Route planning, scheduling, pricing, and resource allocation algorithms.

faq.question: Do we need large datasets for applied AI?
faq.answer: It depends on the problem. Some techniques work with modest data. We assess data requirements during scoping and recommend approaches that fit your data reality.

faq.question: How accurate are forecasting models?
faq.answer: Accuracy depends on data quality and the domain. We provide confidence intervals and error metrics so you know exactly how reliable the forecasts are.

pages.name: AI for Content & Marketing
pages.title: AI Content & Marketing Consulting UK — Scalable Content Generation
pages.description: AI-powered content creation and marketing automation. Scale content production while maintaining quality and brand voice.
pages.intro: AI can scale your content production dramatically — but only if it is set up properly. We build content systems that maintain quality and brand voice at volume.

sections.heading: Content at Scale
sections.body: Product descriptions, blog posts, email campaigns, social media content. We build AI pipelines that produce high-quality content at volumes your team could never match manually — with human review built into the workflow.

sections.heading: Marketing Automation
sections.body: Lead scoring, campaign personalisation, customer segmentation, attribution modelling. AI that makes your marketing spend work harder by targeting the right people with the right message.

sections.heading: Brand Voice Consistency
sections.body: We fine-tune and configure AI systems to match your brand voice. Every piece of content sounds like your company, not a generic AI output.

sections.heading: Quality Controls
sections.body: Every content pipeline includes automated quality checks, plagiarism detection, factual verification, and human review stages. We build for quality, not just speed.

features.title: Content Pipeline
features.description: Automated production of product descriptions, articles, and marketing copy.

features.title: Brand Voice Tuning
features.description: AI configured to match your brand's tone, style, and messaging.

features.title: Quality Automation
features.description: Automated checks for quality, accuracy, and brand compliance.

features.title: Marketing Personalisation
features.description: AI-driven customer segmentation and message targeting.

features.title: Campaign Optimisation
features.description: Automated testing and optimisation of marketing campaigns.

features.title: Analytics & Attribution
features.description: AI-powered marketing analytics and multi-touch attribution.

faq.question: Will AI content hurt our SEO?
faq.answer: Not when done properly. Google penalises low-quality content, not AI-generated content. We build systems that produce content search engines value — original, useful, and well-structured.

faq.question: How do you maintain brand voice?
faq.answer: We train the AI on examples of your existing content, create detailed style guides, and include human review in the pipeline.
